Minced pork and beef baked pie

english: Minced meat pie with mashed potato topping
Minced pork and beef baked pie

ontology.foodex2_description: Meat pie — minced meat with mashed potato topping; closest FoodEx2 anchor for shepherd's pie composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for shepherd's pie; A0FKP (meat-based composite dish) used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for shepherd's pie as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of shepherd's pie is approximately 200g, equivalent to one generous portion served as a main meal.

nutrient_source.title: Shepherd's Pie — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Shepherd's Pie (minced pork/beef baked with mashed potato). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for shepherd's pie (NDB No. 22904) as a secondary reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for shepherd's pie in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being mashed potato (GI ~70–80 for boiled/mashed potato per University of Sydney GI Database) diluted by the meat filling (approximately 50% of dish weight), yielding a blended dish GI of approximately 65. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (22g per 200g serving) / 100 = 14.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 462 mg per 100g; a 200g serving delivers approximately 924 mg sodium, representing 40–46% of the recommended daily limit of 2000–2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit shepherd's pie to a maximum of 150g per occasion and request preparation with reduced-salt stock, no added Worcestershire sauce, and herbs substituted for salt-based seasonings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Mashed potato topping contributes a moderate-to-high GI carbohydrate load; estimated dish GI of 65 with 11g carbohydrate per 100g and 1.8g dietary fibre provides partial glycaemic buffering.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portions to 150–200g, request a thinner potato topping or cauliflower mash substitution, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to reduce the overall glycaemic load of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from red meat and butter estimated at approximately 2.1g per 100g; combined with high sodium (462 mg/100g), regular consumption may contribute to cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit shepherd's pie to occasional consumption, request preparation with lean mince, minimal butter in the mash, and low-sodium stock, and keep portions to 150g or less.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (lamb/beef) is high in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies inflammatory pathways; potato is a nightshade vegetable that may trigger individual RA flares; sodium at 462 mg/100g exceeds the 400 mg RA threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or minimise shepherd's pie made with red meat, consider substituting with turkey or lentil filling, and monitor for nightshade sensitivity from the potato topping.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shepherd's pie is a classic British baked savoury dish consisting of a layer of seasoned minced meat — traditionally lamb, though beef or a pork-beef blend is common in Singapore — topped with a thick layer of creamy mashed potato and baked until golden. The meat filling is typically cooked with onions, carrots, peas, stock, and Worcestershire sauce before being transferred to a baking dish and covered with mashed potato. The dish is then baked at high heat to form a lightly browned crust on the potato surface. In Singapore, shepherd's pie is commonly prepared with a minced pork and beef mixture, reflecting local ingredient availability and taste preferences, and is served as a main course at home, in school canteens, and in Western-style casual restaurants. Nutritionally, a 100g serving provides 116 kcal, 6.4g protein, 5.2g fat, and 11g carbohydrate with 1.8g dietary fibre. Sodium content is high at 462 mg per 100g, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The mashed potato topping contributes a moderate-to-high glycaemic index, estimated at approximately 65 for the composite dish, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish contains dairy (butter in the mash) and may contain gluten from stock or Worcestershire sauce. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following a halal diet unless prepared with halal-certified meat.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as a secondary reference. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database mashed potato values adjusted for meat dilution, which reduces overall confidence for the GI component to medium; however, the overall record confidence remains high due to the strong primary source for all other fields.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific shepherd's pie preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for shepherd's pie as of 2026. Saturated fat (2.1g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 22904, Shepherd's pie, frozen entree) as the closest approved-source equivalent; this is documented as an adapted value. The input record described the dish as 'halal' in dietary_tags; however, the standard preparation of shepherd's pie uses Worcestershire sauce (which may contain anchovies) and the meat sourcing is not specified as halal-certified, so is_halal has been set to false pending confirmation of halal-certified preparation. The input food_group was 'Snacks' and food_subgroup was 'Pies and pastries', which have been preserved from the HPB source classification. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for shepherd's pie exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: 65 × 22g carbohydrate per 200g serving / 100 = 14.3.
